steps.title: Use the genre caution
steps.content: Mooney classified some Cherokee stories as "wonder stories" because they included detailed geographic locations. The caution is that Cherokee people are not known to have applied genre divisions before Mooney's work, so his categories should not be assumed to reflect the elders' own way of distinguishing stories.

steps.title: Reject unsupported doubts
steps.content: A speculates about stories the elders did not share. B shifts to whether the geographic details were accurate, which is not the issue. C speculates about whether Long influenced Mooney's classifications. The supported conclusion concerns the elders' likely agreement with Mooney's genre distinction. The answer is D.
